seo.title: For Homeowners & Rental Operators | ArdenNest
seo.description: How ArdenNest became visible to AI assistants — and what other rental operators can learn from it.

title: From vacation rental to discoverable business
intro: I'm Christophe — co-owner of ArdenNest together with Bieke. But I'm not just a rental operator. I trained as a programmer at the Lernout & Hauspie Speech Products Academy, worked for years as a developer and technical consultant at Wolters Kluwer Financial Services, and today I work as a ClickUp Expert and process automation specialist at Fourcast. Technology and digital strategy are my profession, not my hobby. My hobbies are foraging and preparing mushrooms, building log cabins, chopping wood, keeping chickens, tending the vegetable garden — and being extremely creative in giving children the space to play and grow in our garden. Self-sufficiency in the Ardennes, you could say.
intro2: In early 2026, I completely rewrote this website — not for Google, but for the way people search for information today: through AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude. What I learned along the way, I want to share with other rental operators and small business owners.

learnings.subtitle: What I learned while optimising ArdenNest

answers.title: Answer instead of list
answers.text: Our old site listed features. The new one answers the questions people actually ask: "Is cooking for 26 a problem?" or "Do I need a shower schedule?" AI assistants work with answers, not bullet points.

concrete.title: Concrete beats vague
concrete.text: "Large kitchen" says nothing. "2 Miele Professional dishwashers, 26 plates clean in 30 minutes" is what an AI quotes when someone asks about a holiday home with a professional kitchen.

honesty.title: Dare to name what you don't have
honesty.text: ArdenNest has no swimming pool and no sauna — by design. By explaining that on the site as a deliberate choice rather than staying silent, an AI can pass on that context. Silence counts against you; an explanation becomes an argument.

consistency.title: Consistency is everything
consistency.text: If your site says "26 people" but your Google listing says "20 people", an AI doesn't know which is correct — and picks a competitor where the numbers do match.

geoScan.subtitle: geo-scan.be — check how AI sees your business
geoScan.intro: Based on what I learned at ArdenNest, I built geo-scan.be: a free scan that measures how well your website is readable for AI assistants. The tool checks six pillars:

pillars.readability: Structural readability — can an AI technically read and understand your site?
pillars.identity: Entity identity — does an AI know who you are and what you do?
pillars.answerability: Content answerability — does your site answer the questions people ask?
pillars.authority: Source authority — is your business confirmed by external sources?
pillars.specificity: Factual specificity — does your site contain concrete, verifiable facts?
pillars.consistency: Consistency — is your information correct everywhere?

closing: The scan is free and without obligation. No account needed, no sales pitch — just insight into how AI sees your business today. Questions? Send me a message via the
attribution: geo-scan.be is an initiative by Christophe, co-owner of ArdenNest.
